fields.summary: [CSI] Add ability to publish/unpublish volumes on node managers
Support Container Storage Interface (CSI) in YARN

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: We need to add ability to publish volumes on node managers in staging area, under NM's local dir. And then mount the path to docker container to make it visible in the container.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

Thanks [~cheersyang]

Few comments:
 # Could u pls add javadoc comments in the protocol, publish/unpublish request/response interfaces for apis.
 # Could we use NM_CSI_DRIVER_PREFIX before NM_CSI_ADAPTOR_PREFIX so that "-adaptor" can be avoided?
 # CsiUtils -> CsiConfigUtils if its only config related management in this class?
 # In proto, if NodePublishVolumeRequest is optional, could that be a last param to ensure all required are in first part?
 # Adding to #2 its tough to understand different b/w csi-driver-adaptor and csi-driver. Could we make it more intuitive?
 # I think at Resource level we should get all the noncountable resource info list. with this, we can improve {{prepareCsiVolumes}}
 # Could we need a DeletionService kind of model to get a successful cleanupCsiVolumes? How stable is cleanup from CSI side?
 # Changes for TestDelegatingLinuxContainerRuntime is not needed.

Hi [~sunilg]

I agree with most of your comments, and will upload a new patch to address them. The only exception is
{quote}In proto, if NodePublishVolumeRequest is optional, could that be a last param to ensure all required are in first part?
{quote}
I prefer current order in the patch because that is align with the public CSI protocol, will be easier to maintain.

And your suggestion #7, allow me sometime to investigate if we can move these code to some DeletionTask. Thanks for the suggestion.

Hi [~sunilg]

Could you pls help to review latest patch? Thanks

Thanks [~cheersyang]. Some more comments
 # In CsiAdaptorProtocolService ctor, {{csiDriverName}} value could be const string than a simple plain string.
 # In CsiAdaptorProtocolService#nodePublishVolume, some logs could be debug such as translated message ones.
 # In CsiClientImpl, could CsiGrpcClient be cached?
 # In NodePublishVolumeRequestProtoTranslator, cap and cap1 names could be more clearer.
 # recordContainerCsiVolumesRootDir, i saw a todo. Is that persistence needed? Will RM have a cache of allocated and mapped volumes?

Hi [~sunilg]

Thanks for the review.
{quote}{{csiDriverName}} value could be const string than a simple plain string.
{quote}
{{csiDriverName}} should be a configurabled value, I have a TODO there. I plan to get it done in YARN-9086, the AUX service for csi-driver-adaptors will be a set of services, in order to support multiple drivers. And their driver names will be loading from configuration. Here is to let code work with ch.ctrox.csi.s3-driver.
{quote} some logs could be debug such as translated message ones.
{quote}
Sure. Changed to DEBUG level.
{quote}could CsiGrpcClient be cached?
{quote}
 

The client once created, it will create a netty channel and connect to a socket address, so if we cache it that will waste some resource. Since volume related call won't be very frequent, I think we are fine to create it on the fly.
{quote}cap and cap1 names could be more clearer.
{quote}
Yes, I renamed \{{cap1}} to \{{csiVolumeCap}}.
{quote}Is that persistence needed?
{quote}
I think so. This is on the node manager side, on RM side we are stateless. Because CSI protocol ensures each calls are *idempotent*. That means, e.g we can call publish volume multiple times for a given volume and it will return us success all the time. This design makes us easier to do recovery. However on the NM side, as we need to memorize the location of the local volume root, we need to store that data in the container. Then we need to do persistent so we are able to retrieve it on recovery.

I have uploaded a new patch, pls take a look.

Thanks.

Few comments:
 # Could u pls add javadoc comments in the protocol, publish/unpublish request/response interfaces for apis.
 # Could we use NM_CSI_DRIVER_PREFIX before NM_CSI_ADAPTOR_PREFIX so that "-adaptor" can be avoided?
 # CsiUtils -> CsiConfigUtils if its only config related management in this class?
 # In proto, if NodePublishVolumeRequest is optional, could that be a last param to ensure all required are in first part?
 # Adding to #2 its tough to understand different b/w csi-driver-adaptor and csi-driver. Could we make it more intuitive?
 # I think at Resource level we should get all the noncountable resource info list. with this, we can improve {{prepareCsiVolumes}}
 # Could we need a DeletionService kind of model to get a successful cleanupCsiVolumes? How stable is cleanup from CSI side?
 # Changes for TestDelegatingLinuxContainerRuntime is not needed.

Hi [~sunilg]

I agree with most of your comments, and will upload a new patch to address them. The only exception is
{quote}In proto, if NodePublishVolumeRequest is optional, could that be a last param to ensure all required are in first part?
{quote}
I prefer current order in the patch because that is align with the public CSI protocol, will be easier to maintain.

And your suggestion #7, allow me sometime to investigate if we can move these code to some DeletionTask. Thanks for the suggestion.

Hi [~sunilg]

Could you pls help to review latest patch? Thanks

Thanks [~cheersyang]. Some more comments
 # In CsiAdaptorProtocolService ctor, {{csiDriverName}} value could be const string than a simple plain string.
 # In CsiAdaptorProtocolService#nodePublishVolume, some logs could be debug such as translated message ones.
 # In CsiClientImpl, could CsiGrpcClient be cached?
 # In NodePublishVolumeRequestProtoTranslator, cap and cap1 names could be more clearer.
 # recordContainerCsiVolumesRootDir, i saw a todo. Is that persistence needed? Will RM have a cache of allocated and mapped volumes?

Hi [~sunilg]

Thanks for the review.
{quote}{{csiDriverName}} value could be const string than a simple plain string.
{quote}
{{csiDriverName}} should be a configurabled value, I have a TODO there. I plan to get it done in YARN-9086, the AUX service for csi-driver-adaptors will be a set of services, in order to support multiple drivers. And their driver names will be loading from configuration. Here is to let code work with ch.ctrox.csi.s3-driver.
{quote} some logs could be debug such as translated message ones.
{quote}
Sure. Changed to DEBUG level.
{quote}could CsiGrpcClient be cached?
{quote}
 

The client once created, it will create a netty channel and connect to a socket address, so if we cache it that will waste some resource. Since volume related call won't be very frequent, I think we are fine to create it on the fly.
{quote}cap and cap1 names could be more clearer.
{quote}
Yes, I renamed \{{cap1}} to \{{csiVolumeCap}}.
{quote}Is that persistence needed?
{quote}
I think so. This is on the node manager side, on RM side we are stateless. Because CSI protocol ensures each calls are *idempotent*. That means, e.g we can call publish volume multiple times for a given volume and it will return us success all the time. This design makes us easier to do recovery. However on the NM side, as we need to memorize the location of the local volume root, we need to store that data in the container. Then we need to do persistent so we are able to retrieve it on recovery.

I have uploaded a new patch, pls take a look.

Thanks.
